::: notes
We compare the parallel-planes model (`hers_lm1`: `LDL ~ age + BMI`)
and the interaction model (`hers_lm2`: `LDL ~ age + BMI + age:BMI`)
using a likelihood ratio test (LRT).
A small p-value indicates that the interaction term significantly improves model fit.
:::

#### Comparing HERS models using LRT

::: {#tbl-hers-lrt}

```{r}
#| code-fold: true
lmtest::lrtest(hers_lm1, hers_lm2)
```

Likelihood ratio test comparing the `hers` parallel-planes (`hers_lm1`) and interaction (`hers_lm2`) models

:::


Here, the likelihood ratio test is not statistically significant
(@tbl-hers-lrt: $\chi^2_1 \approx 1.4$, $p \approx 0.24$):
the age-BMI interaction does not fit the data meaningfully better
than we would expect from adding one parameter by chance.
So we prefer the simpler parallel-planes model
(`hers_lm1`, @eq-hers-parallel) on parsimony grounds,
consistent with the AIC and BIC comparison in @tbl-hers-aic-bic.
